hindiTranslation: जिसके अनेक मुख और नेत्र हैं, अनेक तहरके अद्भुत दर्शन हैं, अनेक दिव्य आभूषण हैं, हाथोंमें उठाये हुए अनेक दिव्य आयुध हैं तथा जिनके गलेमें दिव्य मालाएँ हैं, जो दिव्य वस्त्र पहने हुए हैं, जिनके ललाट तथा शरीरपर दिव्य चन्दन, कुंकुम आदि लगा हुआ है, ऐसे सम्पूर्ण आश्चर्यमय, अनन्त रूपोंवाले तथा चारों तरफ मुखवाले देवअपने दिव्य स्वरूप को भगवान् ने दिखाया ॥
englishTranslation: Wearing divine garlands and garments, anointed with divine fragrances, wondrous in every way, endless, and facing everywhere.
essence: The divine presence is beautiful, boundless, and impossible to face from only one side.
speaker: Arjuna
simpleMeaning: Krishna's cosmic form is covered in divine garlands, clothes, and fragrance. It is endless, wondrous, and faces in every direction.
simpleInsight: Awe deepens when the divine is felt as everywhere, not contained in one shape.
detailedMeaning: On Kurukshetra, Arjuna is still staring at Krishna's cosmic form after the revelation begins. The form is not just vast; it is adorned, living, and impossible to contain. This verse adds to the shock before Arjuna's fear and prayer.
modernRelevance: You stand speechless in front of something too vast to fit your plans — a birth, a loss, a revelation. The mind stops arguing when reality becomes larger than your frame.
translationLiteral: दिव्य (divine) / माल्य (garlands) / अम्बर (garments) / धरम् (wearing) / दिव्य (divine) / गन्ध (fragrance) / अनुलेपनम् (anointing) / सर्व (all) / आश्चर्य (wonder) / मयम् (filled with) / देवम् (the radiant one) / अनन्तम् (endless) / विश्वतोमुखम् (facing everywhere)
